Command →
read(0) → Client
→ Frame → MCU
→ Handler → ACK

div.style: color: rgb(194, 65, 12) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
← Telemetry
Sensor → Pack
→ Frame → Parse
→ EventBus → Module

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
color: rgb(126, 34, 206) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
📄 my_app.py
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
from robot_host import Robot, Runtime
from my_sensor import MySensorModule
async with Robot('/dev/tty') as robot:
sensor = MySensorModule()
runtime = Runtime(robot, tick_hz=50)
runtime.add_module(sensor)
await robot.arm()
val = await sensor.read(0)
await runtime.run(duration=10)

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
color: rgb(30, 64, 175) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
📄 my_sensor.py
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
class MySensorModule(BaseModule):
name = 'my_sensor'
def topics(self):
return ['telemetry.my_sensor']
def on_telemetry_my_sensor(self, d):
self.value = d['value']
async def read(self, id):
return await self._robot
.client.cmd_my_sensor(id)
